targets.rawSql: SELECT date_trunc('minute', created_at) AS time, status, COUNT(*) AS count FROM ingestion_jobs WHERE created_at >= $__timeFrom() AND created_at <= $__timeTo() GROUP BY 1, 2 ORDER BY 1
SELECT status, COUNT(*) AS count FROM ingestion_jobs GROUP BY status
SELECT COUNT(*) AS value FROM articles WHERE ingested_at >= $__timeFrom() AND ingested_at <= $__timeTo()
SELECT COUNT(*) AS value FROM chunks
SELECT date_trunc('minute', started_at) AS time, request_type, AVG(duration_ms) AS avg_ms FROM model_call_logs WHERE started_at >= $__timeFrom() AND started_at <= $__timeTo() GROUP BY 1, 2 ORDER BY 1
SELECT status, COUNT(*) AS count FROM model_call_logs WHERE started_at >= $__timeFrom() AND started_at <= $__timeTo() GROUP BY status

SELECT started_at, request_type, model_name, prompt_template, duration_ms, status, retries FROM model_call_logs WHERE started_at >= $__timeFrom() AND started_at <= $__timeTo() ORDER BY started_at DESC LIMIT 100

SELECT date_trunc('minute', created_at) AS time, AVG(retrieval_latency_ms) AS retrieval_ms, AVG(answer_latency_ms) AS answer_ms FROM query_logs WHERE created_at >= $__timeFrom() AND created_at <= $__timeTo() GROUP BY 1 ORDER BY 1
SELECT ROUND(100.0 * SUM(CASE WHEN aborted THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) / NULLIF(COUNT(*), 0), 1) AS value FROM query_logs WHERE created_at >= $__timeFrom() AND created_at <= $__timeTo()
SELECT COUNT(*) AS value FROM query_logs WHERE created_at >= $__timeFrom() AND created_at <= $__timeTo()

SELECT created_at, question, answer, aborted, abort_reason, retrieval_latency_ms, answer_latency_ms FROM query_logs WHERE created_at >= $__timeFrom() AND created_at <= $__timeTo() ORDER BY created_at DESC LIMIT 50

SELECT j.created_at, j.input_value, j.status, j.source, a.title FROM ingestion_jobs j LEFT JOIN articles a ON a.url = j.input_value OR a.canonical_url = j.input_value WHERE j.created_at >= $__timeFrom() AND j.created_at <= $__timeTo() ORDER BY j.created_at DESC LIMIT 50

SELECT started_at, request_type, model_name, prompt_template, duration_ms, status, LEFT(prompt_input, 600) AS prompt_preview, LEFT(response_output, 600) AS response_preview FROM model_call_logs WHERE started_at >= $__timeFrom() AND started_at <= $__timeTo() ORDER BY started_at DESC LIMIT 50

panels.title: QA Retrieval Trace — Evidence Selected per Query

targets.rawSql: SELECT q.created_at, LEFT(q.question, 120) AS question, e->>'article' AS article, e->>'url' AS url, ROUND((e->>'rrf_score')::numeric, 5) AS rrf_score, LEFT(e->>'content', 300) AS content_preview FROM query_logs q CROSS JOIN jsonb_array_elements(q.retrieval_trace->'evidence') AS e WHERE q.created_at >= $__timeFrom() AND q.created_at <= $__timeTo() AND q.retrieval_trace IS NOT NULL ORDER BY q.created_at DESC, rrf_score DESC LIMIT 100

panels.title: QA Retrieval Trace — Vector vs BM25 Hits

targets.rawSql: SELECT q.created_at, LEFT(q.question, 100) AS question, 'vector' AS source, v->>'article' AS article, ROUND((v->>'score')::numeric, 4) AS score, LEFT(v->>'content', 200) AS content_preview FROM query_logs q CROSS JOIN jsonb_array_elements(q.retrieval_trace->'vector_hits') AS v WHERE q.created_at >= $__timeFrom() AND q.created_at <= $__timeTo() AND q.retrieval_trace IS NOT NULL UNION ALL SELECT q.created_at, LEFT(q.question, 100), 'bm25', b->>'article', ROUND((b->>'score')::numeric, 4), LEFT(b->>'content', 200) FROM query_logs q CROSS JOIN jsonb_array_elements(q.retrieval_trace->'bm25_hits') AS b WHERE q.created_at >= $__timeFrom() AND q.created_at <= $__timeTo() AND q.retrieval_trace IS NOT NULL ORDER BY created_at DESC, source, score DESC LIMIT 200

panels.title: QA Debug Inspector — Question vs Retrieved Evidence
panels.description: One row per query. model_refused=true means the LLM saw the evidence but decided it was irrelevant (aborted=false means the code sufficiency check passed — the mismatch between these two flags is the signal).

targets.rawSql: SELECT q.created_at, LEFT(q.question, 120) AS question, q.aborted, q.abort_reason, CASE WHEN q.answer ILIKE '%cannot answer%' THEN true ELSE false END AS model_refused, LEFT(q.answer, 150) AS model_response, (SELECT string_agg(e->>'article', ' | ' ORDER BY (e->>'rrf_score')::numeric DESC) FROM jsonb_array_elements(q.retrieval_trace->'evidence') e) AS retrieved_articles, (SELECT string_agg(ROUND((e->>'rrf_score')::numeric, 4)::text, ' | ' ORDER BY (e->>'rrf_score')::numeric DESC) FROM jsonb_array_elements(q.retrieval_trace->'evidence') e) AS rrf_scores FROM query_logs q WHERE q.created_at >= $__timeFrom() AND q.created_at <= $__timeTo() AND q.retrieval_trace IS NOT NULL ORDER BY q.created_at DESC LIMIT 30

panels.title: QA Refusal Rate — Code Abort vs Model Refused

targets.rawSql: SELECT date_trunc('hour', created_at) AS time, COUNT(*) FILTER (WHERE aborted = true) AS code_aborted, COUNT(*) FILTER (WHERE aborted = false AND answer ILIKE '%cannot answer%') AS model_refused, COUNT(*) FILTER (WHERE aborted = false AND answer NOT ILIKE '%cannot answer%') AS answered FROM query_logs WHERE created_at >= $__timeFrom() AND created_at <= $__timeTo() GROUP BY 1 ORDER BY 1

panels.title: QA Decomposition — Raw Ask vs Search Query vs Directives
panels.description: How each question was split. search_query is what actually gets embedded/reranked; answer_directives are routed to the generation prompt only. analysis_fell_back=true means the LLM analyzer failed and a deterministic strip was used.

targets.rawSql: SELECT q.created_at, LEFT(q.question, 90) AS raw_question, q.retrieval_trace->'query_analysis'->>'search_query' AS search_query, q.retrieval_trace->'query_analysis'->>'keywords' AS keywords, q.retrieval_trace->'query_analysis'->>'answer_directives' AS answer_directives, q.retrieval_trace->'query_analysis'->>'intent' AS intent, q.retrieval_trace->'query_analysis'->>'fell_back' AS analysis_fell_back FROM query_logs q WHERE q.created_at >= $__timeFrom() AND q.created_at <= $__timeTo() AND q.retrieval_trace IS NOT NULL ORDER BY q.created_at DESC LIMIT 50

panels.title: QA Gate Decisions — Why each query answered or refused
panels.description: decision = answer/refuse. stage = which gate decided (recall_floor, rerank, evidence_sufficiency, url_lookup, answered). top_rerank is the best SLM relevance score (gate refuses below qa_min_rerank_score).

targets.rawSql: SELECT q.created_at, LEFT(q.question, 80) AS question, q.retrieval_trace->'gate'->>'decision' AS decision, q.retrieval_trace->'gate'->>'stage' AS stage, ROUND((q.retrieval_trace->'gate'->>'best_vector')::numeric, 3) AS best_vector, ROUND((q.retrieval_trace->'gate'->>'best_bm25')::numeric, 3) AS best_bm25, ROUND((q.retrieval_trace->'gate'->>'top_rerank')::numeric, 3) AS top_rerank, LEFT(q.retrieval_trace->'gate'->>'reason', 140) AS reason FROM query_logs q WHERE q.created_at >= $__timeFrom() AND q.created_at <= $__timeTo() AND q.retrieval_trace IS NOT NULL ORDER BY q.created_at DESC LIMIT 50

panels.title: QA Reranker — Candidate Scores (rerank vs RRF)
panels.description: Every fused candidate with its SLM relevance score. Watch for flat/low rerank_score across all candidates — that is the 'I don't really have this' signal that the old RRF-only gate missed.

targets.rawSql: SELECT q.created_at, LEFT(q.question, 80) AS question, f->>'article' AS article, ROUND((f->>'rrf_score')::numeric, 5) AS rrf_score, ROUND((f->>'rerank_score')::numeric, 3) AS rerank_score, LEFT(f->>'url', 70) AS url FROM query_logs q CROSS JOIN jsonb_array_elements(q.retrieval_trace->'fused') AS f WHERE q.created_at >= $__timeFrom() AND q.created_at <= $__timeTo() AND q.retrieval_trace IS NOT NULL ORDER BY q.created_at DESC, rerank_score DESC NULLS LAST LIMIT 200

SELECT date_trunc('minute', created_at) AS time, AVG((retrieval_trace->'stage_latencies_ms'->>'analysis')::numeric) AS analysis_ms, AVG((retrieval_trace->'stage_latencies_ms'->>'retrieval')::numeric) AS retrieval_ms, AVG((retrieval_trace->'stage_latencies_ms'->>'rerank')::numeric) AS rerank_ms, AVG((retrieval_trace->'stage_latencies_ms'->>'answer')::numeric) AS answer_ms FROM query_logs WHERE created_at >= $__timeFrom() AND created_at <= $__timeTo() AND retrieval_trace IS NOT NULL GROUP BY 1 ORDER BY 1

SELECT q.retrieval_trace->'gate'->>'stage' AS stage, COUNT(*) AS count FROM query_logs q WHERE q.created_at >= $__timeFrom() AND q.created_at <= $__timeTo() AND q.aborted = true AND q.retrieval_trace IS NOT NULL GROUP BY 1 ORDER BY count DESC

panels.title: QA Degradation — Analyzer & Reranker Fallbacks
panels.description: When the SLM analyzer or reranker fails, the pipeline degrades (deterministic strip / RRF order). Spikes here mean the small models are struggling and answers are running on the fallback path.

targets.rawSql: SELECT date_trunc('hour', created_at) AS time, COUNT(*) FILTER (WHERE retrieval_trace->'query_analysis'->>'fell_back' = 'true') AS analysis_fallback, COUNT(*) FILTER (WHERE retrieval_trace->>'rerank_fell_back' = 'true') AS rerank_fallback FROM query_logs WHERE created_at >= $__timeFrom() AND created_at <= $__timeTo() AND retrieval_trace IS NOT NULL GROUP BY 1 ORDER BY 1
